label: volatile
summary: Gold may react strongly because CPI can shift real-yield and USD expectations.
reasoning: Hot CPI can support higher-for-longer rate expectations, usually pressuring non-yielding assets like gold.
Cold CPI can weaken rate expectations and support gold if USD/yields fall.
The safest MVP label is volatility because direction depends on the surprise versus forecast.
riskWarning: Do not treat this as a buy/sell signal. Wait for actual data and risk controls.

label: volatile
summary: Bitcoin may react to liquidity expectations and risk appetite after the Fed decision.
reasoning: Dovish language can improve risk appetite and liquidity expectations.
Hawkish language can pressure risk assets through stronger yields and USD.
Crypto often reacts sharply around central bank communication.
riskWarning: Educational market intelligence only. Crypto volatility can be extreme.

label: bullish_or_bearish_depending_on_surprise
summary: Oil direction depends on whether OPEC signals tighter or looser supply than expected.
reasoning: Production cuts or extended cuts usually support oil prices.
Unexpected supply increases or weak demand commentary can pressure oil.
Inventory and demand context can change the final reaction.
riskWarning: Energy headlines can reverse quickly when details are clarified.
